UF-168-1.title: Lean toolchain (v4.29.1) / Mathlib (v4.30.0-rc1) version mismatch surfaces as next blocker after slot 148 success
UF-168-1.discovered_by: slot 148 Path A post-fix lake build
UF-168-1.description: Project lean-toolchain file pins 'leanprover/lean4:stable' (resolves to v4.29.1 on operator machine). Mathlib pinned at git rev 0c154d67103f74be3a0f2c509f72ccbf5be9f2a7 (tag v4.30.0-rc1) which itself requires lean v4.30.0-rc1. Major-rev mismatch causes 6 upstream compile errors in Batteries / Mathlib / ProofWidgets (Unknown constant Lean.Parser.Tactic.Grind.«grind·_», Unknown constant List.Sublist.cons_cons, Unknown identifier foldr_eq_foldl, etc.). The mismatch is PRE-EXISTING (visible in pre-fix log build_log_pre_148R.txt, not caused by slot 148 Path A); slot 148 success simply moves it to the front of the failure queue.
UF-168-1.downstream_impact: Blocks Phase C.1-C.3 iterative work on WallisFamily.lean because Lake cannot compile Mathlib far enough to reach the project's lean_lib target. M10 V0 Lean chain remains gated on toolchain alignment.
recommended_remediation: Path α (recommended): Bump lean/lean-toolchain to leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0-rc1 + elan toolchain install + lake clean + lake build
Path β: Downgrade Mathlib pin to v4.29.x in lakefile.lean (risky; v4.29 may lack Asymptotics/Defs.lean)
Path γ: Bisect to a custom pinned Mathlib rev that builds clean on Lean v4.29.1 and contains the restructured Asymptotics directory
UF-168-1.fire_recommendation: Fire as slot 148.B (or slot 149) immediately; high-leverage prerequisite for Phase C.1-C.3 + M10 V0 chain unblock

UF-168-2.title: Lake manifest-out-of-date warning persists across builds
UF-168-2.discovered_by: slot 148 Path A post-fix lake build (also present in pre-fix log)
UF-168-2.description: Lake emits 'warning: manifest out of date: git revision of dependency mathlib changed; use lake update mathlib to update it' at every build invocation. Inspection: lake-manifest.json records mathlib rev 0c154d67103f74be3a0f2c509f72ccbf5be9f2a7, which matches the on-disk mathlib HEAD (git rev-parse HEAD returns the same SHA). The warning may be a stale-cache artifact or a different manifest field is out of date; non-blocking.
UF-168-2.downstream_impact: Cosmetic; build proceeds despite the warning. Will likely auto-resolve when operator runs lake update mathlib as part of UF-168-1 remediation.
UF-168-2.recommended_remediation: Run lake update mathlib once at UF-168-1 Path α/β/γ remediation time
